The question describes an experiment where plants native to Maryland were placed in an environment very different from their natural habitat—specifically, one that mimics desert conditions with hot temperatures and limited water. The fact that most of the plants did not survive after five weeks points to the difficulty that plants (or any organisms) face when subjected to severe changes in their environment. This is a common phenomenon in biology, where organisms have specific adaptations to their native environments, and drastic changes can lead to their inability to survive. Therefore, the best explanation is that few plants survive severe changes to their environment.
